sections.paragraph:
ἁπλόω (s. ἁπλοῦς; Hero Alex. III p. 130, 7; Cornutus 32 p. 66, 7; Soranus p. 76, 16; Aelian, NA 12, 27; Ps.-Callisth. p. 36, 10; Herm. Wr. Fgm. XXIII 52 [488, 6 Sc.]; Anth. Pal. 11, 107, 4; also ἁπλώσῃς τὴν ὁδόν σου [ἀπώσῃς BS*] Job 22:3; νεκρὰν ἡπλωμένην TestJob 40:8; ἥπλωσε τὰς πτέρυγας αὐτοῦ GrBar 6:7; ἁπλώσας τὰς χείρας of hands outstretched in a receptive gesture ApcMos 37) make single, unfold pass. in act. sense (TestJob 40:8; Aelian, NA 14, 26) χάρις ἁπλουμένη unfolding grace, i.e. generosity that is constantly reaching out Dg 11:5.

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

Soranus Soranus , I–II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

act. act. = active

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 104
